our_predictions.ism_jaspar: risk T strengthens MEF2 motif; attribution also points at adjacent PU.1/SPI1 element

paper.title: Context-dependent regulatory variants in Alzheimer's disease

measured_mpra.prior_mpra: As reported by the 2025 context-dependent preprint (PMC12265656): PREVIOUS MPRA assays in HEK293T and THP-1 cells showed NO significant allele-specific effect for rs6733839 (subtle upregulation noted). The preprint does not name which prior study; NOT attributed to Cooper 2022.

measured_findings_rs6733839.snp_centered_mpra: no significant allele-specific effect in THP-1 macrophages, brain tissue, or HEK293T
measured_findings_rs6733839.peak_centered_mpra: T allele drives transcriptional REPRESSION in THP-1 macrophages and HMC3 microglia-like cells
measured_findings_rs6733839.brain_mpra: upregulation detected in brain MPRA constructs (T allele)
measured_findings_rs6733839.ml_models_in_paper: predicted INCREASED chromatin accessibility for T allele in both macrophages and brain
measured_findings_rs6733839.mechanism: immune models -> T strengthens SPI1 motif -> repression in immune cells; brain models -> MEF2 gain-of-function -> upregulation in brain; net effect is context-dependent (repressor combinable with adjacent motifs)

measured_mpra.cooper_2022_role: Cited as the landmark large-scale AD/PSP MPRA (5,706 variants, 320 functional regulatory variants). Full text NOT retrieved; no rs6733839-specific result attributed to it (abstract does not mention rs6733839/BIN1).
